event_info: swing on offer for Arshdeep. Shapes away past the poke from Rickelton. Just away from the outside edge through to the keeper
outswinger outside off; Rickelton waits for the ball and plays it late. Dabs it down to short third man
peach of a delivery from Arshdeep. The seam presentation was so good. Swings away and beats the outside edge of Rickelton; who looks to defend on the front foot
make that another play and miss. Rickelton goes for the drive; the ball swings further away from outside off and makes it hard to get bat on ball. Past the outside edge for the third time this over
finally Rickelton gets good amount of bat on ball. Presses forward and defends it to cover
excellent first over from Arshdeep. Rickelton stays inside the line of the ball and steers it down to third man for a single. Gets off the mark
just a hint of swing from Jansen. Rickelton drops it in front of mid-off for a quick single
what a way to get off the mark from QDK. Reaches out for the wide delivery outside off and goes through the line. Pumps it over cover and it goes all the way
FOUR; more runs. Sprayed down the line. Quinton de Kock misses the tuck and the ball clips the pad and runs down to fine leg fence for four leg-byes
good length delivery on middle and off. Defended solidly off the front foot
excellent delivery from Jansen. Hits the turf and nips back in sharply to beat the inside edge of the bat. Goes through bat-pad gap and flies over the stumps
good length delivery outside off; Quinton de Kock chops the cut to gully

ball_event: out Caught by Shashank Singh!! 100 wickets for Arshdeep Singh in IPL. Arshdeep has a big smile on his face
event_info: he knows this wasn't a great delivery. Sprayed down the leg; Rickelton picks it up off this pads and hits it straight to Shashank Singh at deep square leg. Taken just inside the boundary ropes above his head. Rickelton struck that well but found Shashank perfectly. Ryan Rickelton c Shashank Singh b Arshdeep Singh 2(8)

ball_event: out Caught by Yuzvendra Chahal!! Two in two. Arshdeep Singh is on a roll. Arshdeep gets the ball to shape away from the right-hander. Suryakumar Yadav goes for the drive straightaway. The swing takes the outside edge and flies to short third man. Chahal the fielder
event_info: moves to his left and takes a sharp catch. Punjab fans are dancing in the crowd. Hat-trick ball coming up for Arshdeep Singh. Suryakumar Yadav c Yuzvendra Chahal b Arshdeep Singh 0(1)
attacks the stumps with a length delivery on middle. Naman Dhir tucks it to mid-wicket
Naman Dhir pushes the length delivery outside off to backward point
good length delivery outside off; Naman Dhir leans on the front foot and drives it to cover

hint of shape into the right-hander. Naman Dhir tucks it to mid-wicket and sprints for a quick single. Had to put in a dive to make it to the other end as the throw came in at the bowler's end. Connolly is the fielder; he misses the direct-hit and Naman Dhir makes his ground. Would have been tight had he hit
Xavier Bartlett starts with a yorker. Naman Dhir digs it out to mid-on

full and on the pads; asking to be hit. Naman Dhir accepts gleefully and tucks it through mid-wicket. Raced away along the ground for four. Vyshak dives near mid-wicket but gets a bad bounce and hence can't make the stop
in the air and falls short of the man at sweeper cover. Naman Dhir goes for the shot inside out over cover. He gets a thick outside edge that flies towards the fielder. Goes on the bounce
full and angling across the left-hander. Quinton de Kock drags the ball off the inner half of the blade to deep mid-wicket. Fielder slides and pulls it back inside the boundary
slower delivery on leg-stump; Quinton de Kock tucks it to the on-side and gets to the other end
angling in on the pads; tucked to deep square leg. Shashank Singh runs to his left and puts in a full length dive to save two. Excellent piece of fielding
back of a length delivery outside off; dabbed down to short third man

dropped! That was a sitter. Chahal is the fielder; he hints that the light came in his way. Naman Dhir walks across and scoops the length delivery from outside off. Doesn't hit it well; the ball lobs to short fine leg and is shelled
half-volley outside off; driven down to mid-off
one bounce and over the ropes. Quinton de Kock stands tall and flats the length delivery over mid-off. Not timed well; but has enough to race away
shortish length delivery outside off; Quinton de Kock cuts it wide of point
short and wide outside off; Naman Dhir gets across and cuts it to deep point. Hangs onto the strike with a single

chance of a run-out. Direct-hit would have got QDK. He had given up. Naman Dhir drives it to mid-off and calls 'yes' initially. Shreyas Iyer got to the ball quickly and Naman Dhir had to change his mind. QDK was halfway down by the time he sends him back. Shreyas Iyer misses and QDK gets away
shortish in length on middle; Naman Dhir rocks on the back foot and pulls it over mid-wicket. Few bounces and over the fence
good length delivery outside off; pushed to the off-side
swung away. Into the pads; Naman Dhir heaves it to the on-side. It was a tad short; which made Naman Dhir pick the length and put it away. Beats deep square leg fielder for four
just the kind of over MI was looking for. Length delivery gets pumped up. Naman Dhir clears his front foot and thumps it over long-on for a maximum

another run-out opportunity for Shreyas Iyer. Naman Dhir drives it straight to mid-off and sets off for a quick single. Shreyas Iyer was hanging on the edge of the circle; he picks the ball and fires at the non-strikers end. Misses and Naman Dhir makes his ground
efforless from Naman Dhir. Welcomes Yuzvendra Chahal with a huge hit. Gets a loopy delivery on middle; Naman Dhir gets under the ball and smokes it over long-off
tucks to the on-side for a run
this time it is QDK. Right in the slot; QDK gets low and slog sweeps the flighted delivery over cow corner fence. Not a great start from Chahal

50-run stand comes up with this boundary. Chahal drags the length half way down well outside off; QDK rocks on the back foot and pulls it wide of long-on. No chance for the fielder as it was hit with power
pushed through well outside off; wided
googly outside off; QDK shoulders arms thinking the ball was outside the tramline. Given wide by the umpire. PKBS review and end up with a successful review
cut wide of cover-point for a quick single
half-volley outside off; driven along the ground down to long-on
very full on middle; Naman Dhir digs it out to long-on
slower delivery on middle; Quinton de Kock drops it to vacant mid-wicket region and sprint for a brace. Excellent running from the duo
much fuller outside off; Quinton de Kock squeezes it out to deep point
slower delivery from Bartlett. Naman waits for the ball and cuts it to sweeper cover
good over from Bartlett. It's been slower ones or just short of yorker length this over. Quinton de Kock opens the bat face and dabs it to deep point to a wide yorker

Vyshak is into the attack and is put away. Fuller in length and has plenty of width on offer; Quinton de Kock gets under the ball and pumps it over backward point. No protection behind square and the ball raced away
slower delivery well outside the tramline. Wided
de Kock tries to be cheeky. Goes for the reverse scoop and misses. The ball hits his glove and rolls to the on-side
excellently placed. Angled across the left-hander; de Kock waits for the ball and cuts it late to pick the gap. Through backward point and short third man for four more
yorker outside off; Quinton de Kock pushes it to cover-point and sets off. Had to settle down for a single
full toss and Naman Dhir misses out on the opportunity to put it away. Hits it straight to deep mid-wicket. Just a single
wide yorker from Vyshak. de Kock square drives it to deep point
leg-break on the pads; Quinton de Kock drops it to mid-wicket and calls for two straightaway. Gets it comfortably with some good running
tossed up down the leg; de Kock sinks low and sweeps it fine. Straight to the fielder
well tossed up on leg-stump. Quinton de Kock skips down the ground and nails it over deep mid-wicket. Got to the pitch of the ball in a flash and put it away
driven down to long-on and gets to his 25th IPL fifty. Raises his bat to the crowd and soaks in the applause
tucked to the on-side
wrong 'un on middle. Quinton de Kock gets squared up as he looks to work it on the on-side. Drops it to cover and gets to the other end
how did that miss the stumps? It was full and around off. Quinton de Kock clears his front foot and misses the big slog. The ball sneaks past the off-pole through to the keeper
smart batting from QDK. Gets the width he needs and he steers it wide of backward point. Hurried to the fence
cross seam delivery and in the slot. Quinton de Kock gets in a strong position and heaves it over mid-wicket. Gets it right of the screws and the ball sailed deep into the stands
good length delivery outside off; cut wide of backward point
another full toss missed by Naman Dhir. This time it was the low full toss. Tucked to mid-wicket
yorker outside off; squeezed to point for a run
flighted delivery well outside off; de Kock goes for the reverse sweep and misses. Couldn't get anywhere close to the ball
100-run stand comes up with this monstor. Tossed up wrong'un from Chahal. Quinton de Kock picks it and deposits it over deep square leg with a slog sweep
full and wide outside off; dragged to long-on
now it's Naman Dhir. Skips down the track and gets to the pitch of the ball to hit it inside out over cover. Goes through with the shot and the timing takes the ball well over the boundary rope
looks to charge yet again; Naman Dhir gets too close to the ball. Can't get elevation and he hits it one bounce to long-on
slower through the air; Quinton de Kock hangs on the back foot and pushes it to cover
slower delivery outside off; cut wide of backward point
banged in short at the batter's badge. Pulled to deep square leg
goes for the full delivery on the pads; Quinton de Kock tucks it between deep mid-wicket and square leg. Duo settle for a single
full and angling across. Naman Dhir drags the ball down to long-on
Jansen gets away and a poor delivery down the leg. Comes round the wicket and bowls a full toss outside leg. de Kock fails to get bat on ball
goes for the wide yorker. Quinton de Kock has to drag it from well outside off and hits it to mid-wicket
fullish in length on middle; driven hard down to long-off. Just a single for Naman Dhir

fifty for Naman Dhir. This has been excellent knock. Promoted up the order after Mumbai Indians lost couple of quick wickets; he was the aggressor in this partnership but now has taken a backseat. He raises his bat and acknowledges the applause of the home crowd. Fullish delivery around off; Dhir drags the drive off the inner half to long-on

in the air and safe. Slower ball bowled into the surface; Quinton de Kock has to reach for it and toe-ends the flat-batted hit straight up. Stoinis starts to go for the catch from extra cover but loses sight of the ball because of the light. The ball lands safely behind the non-striker
fuller and outside off; Dhir drives sans timing to extra cover

ball_event: out Caught by Xavier Bartlett!! Shashank has this knack of picking wickets. He did it in the previous game against SRH where he picked up Abhishek and Head in the same over. Now breaks this partnership by sending back a well-set Naman Dhir. On a length and outside off
event_info: Dhir looks to loft on the up; meets the ball on top of the bounce and creams it flat towards long-off. He did not get the elevation and Bartlett does well to jump up; stretches his hands up and take it over his head. That ball was travelling but Bartlett had it under control. Naman Dhir c Xavier Bartlett b Shashank Singh 50(31) [4s-3 6s-3]
back of a length around off; Hardik drops it beside the pitch on the off-side and crosses over
angled across the left-hander; Quinton drives square and retains strike

just eludes the sweeper's run. Full toss outside off; Quinton opens the bat face and slices it square. Priyansh sprints to his right from sweeper but the ball lands wide of him and goes over the rope. There were two fielders in the deep - backward point and sweeper cover. Quinton split them perfectly..
slower ball outside off; not within the marker and Quinton lets it pass; collects the wide
low full toss angled across; Quinton drags the drive towards deep mid-wicket
short of length at the body; Hardik does not have the space to free his hands; gets cramped and mistimes the pull to the vacant mid-wicket region

full toss slanted across; Quinton winds up for the big mow - moves around in the crease and stays deep; crouches his back knee and swings to the leg-side; the ball sneaks under his bat. He knows he's missed out..
very full delivery outside off; Quinton opens the bat face and slices it over point; just a single
short of length delivery; Hardik does not quite middle the pull but gets it away to deep square leg
fuller and outside off; Hardik drives towards extra cover and Stoinis makes a diving stop
short and bowled into the wicket; Hardik rides the bounce and chops it to extra cover

much needed hit for Quinton and Mumbai Indians. There was a quiet period and they needed a move on. Slower ball outside off; plenty of width and Quinton slams it flat over long-off. Flat but high enough to beat a tall Jansen on the rope

back to back sixes. Was it right to give Shashank an extra over? He had broken the partnership. Length delivery around off; Quinton moves across his stumps and gets under it; heaves it high and well over the deep square leg fence
on a length and angled across; Quinton drives to deep backward point
back of a length outside off; Hardik flat-bats it to long-off and retains strike
short of length outside off; does not quite come on and Hardik checks his cut; sends it to sweeper cover
yorker outside off; Quinton can only squeeze it out to sweeper cover
95.5ks off-pace delivery banged into the wicket; Hardik gives everything behind the slap but only toe-ends it towards extra cover. So far so good from Bartlett; just 3 runs
low full toss outside off; Quinton moves across and drives to long-off
nice juicy full toss around knee height; Hardik miscues the heave to deep mid-wicket. He missed out there..

hundred for Quinton de Kock. What a statement! Ignored for the first few games of the season; only brought in because Rohit got injured; lost a couple of early wickets but he has played a magnificent knock. Hardik has his hand up. He's as delighted as Quinton. Coming to the ball: Low full toss outside off; Quinton bends down; uses the pace on the ball and reverse laps it fine of short third. That was some shot to reach the milestone

Hardik gets into the act. Finally; he was struggling to middle the ball but has sent this sailing over the rope. Back of a length delivery; angled across and there was width for Hardik to free his hands; pumps it down the ground and sends it all the way
yorker outside off; Hardik fails to dig it out. It was almost a low full toss which Hardik missed..

ball_event: out Caught by Xavier Bartlett!! Oh my word! This has to be the catch of the season. Even the Mumbai Indians dugout especially Rohit Sharma is stunned by the effort of Shreyas Iyer. The Punjab Kings skipper even gets up and asks the crowd for applause. Jansen angles it across
event_info: takes pace off and makes Hardik reach for the big shot. It comes off the toe-end and skies straight back over the bowler. Iyer runs to his left from long-on and leaps sideways; reverse cups it and is airborne (he was outside the rope) when he throws the ball up to Bartlett. The momentum was taking him over the rope and hence Iyer had to release the ball. He spotted Bartlett nearby and lobbed it straight to him while being in the air. Bartlett's name will be in the scorecard but it was all Iyer. Hardik Pandya c Xavier Bartlett b Marco Jansen 14(12) [6s-1]
what a yorker; not much Rutherford can do about that other than jam it back down the pitch
another pinpoint yorker; Rutherford brings his bat down in time and digs it towards short third
fullish delivery outside off; Quinton eases it to cover and retains strike
whoa! Not the first time he has played that shot today. Arshdeep does not nail the yorker; bowls a full toss outside off and Quinton reverse laps it over short third
fullish delivery around off; Quinton drives to long-off
another perfect yorker and Rutherford jams it back to Arshdeep. Three balls for Rutherford and three excellent yorkers
now the wide yorker; Rutherford thinks it is away from the tramline and lets it pass; but it curls in and lands within the tramline

ball_event: out Bowled!! Cleaned up! Rutherford will walk back thinking that was top stuff. He faced five deliveries and every single one of the them was a yorker. This one tails in slightly
event_info: Rutherford gets his front leg out of the way; but the ball curls past the inside-edge and crashes into the middle-stump. Not much Rutherford could have done with such brilliant execution. Sherfane Rutherford b Arshdeep Singh 1(5)
much needed boundary. Arshdeep for once misses his yorker and sprays a full toss down leg; Tilak sits down inside the line and sweeps it to fine leg. No fielder there and the ball races away..
121.7ks slower ball outside off; Quinton flat-bats it back to Vyshak and he makes the stop
120ks pace off delivery; on the shorter side and Quinton drags the pull in front of square; easy couple
full pitcher outside off; Quinton mistimes the lofted drive to long-off
slower ball bowled into the wicket; Tilak waits for the ball to arrive and pumps the pull in front of square; one bounce and over the ropes. It was the knuckle ball and Vyshak didn't nail it

ball_event: out Tilak Varma Run Out!! Excellent work by Vyshak. First he goes full and wide with his line and length
event_info: Tilak ekes out an inside-edge on his attempted drive; the ball hits him on the body and drops to the off-side. Quinton calls for a quick single but Vyshak is aware. He runs to his right; picks up the ball and scores a direct-hit at the non-striker's end. Tilak was just short of the crease. Tilak Varma run out (Vijaykumar Vyshak) 8(3) [4s-2]
full and angled across; not quite yorker length and Quinton lofts it straight down the ground. Not off the middle and hence the ball does not race away to the fence

full and swinging into the left-hander; Priyansh offers the full face of the bat and eases it down the wicket. Chahar can't make the stop and Thakur is rushed across from mid-on; he moves across and slides to make the stop
Prabhsimran is off the mark with a boundary. Too straight from Chahar - attacks the stumps and offers a freebie on the pads; flicked in front of the square and there's no need to run for that

back to back fours. Short and outside off; there's a deep backward point in place but Prabhsimran did well to evade him; he plays it late and does not overhit the cut shot; opens the bat face and almost guides it behind square
on a length and outside off; Prabhsimran steers it with an open bat face to deep backward point

Priyansh has his first six of the night. Ample amount of width on offer and Priyansh does not back away; throws his bat on the lofted drive and slices it over deep backward point. Just had enough to clear the fielder
sprays it down leg; Priyansh stays inside the line and misses the glance; wide called

expensive first over - 21 runs. He has been very poor with the first over; went for plenty against Rajasthan Royals and has done the same versus Punjab Kings. The knuckle ball is dragged short; Priyansh hangs back and nails the pull over mid-wicket; couple of bounces and over the rope
angling into the pads; Prabhsimran stays back and clips it backward of square
Priyansh continues to deal in boundaries.. Leg-lined delivery and a touch shot from Priyansh; he allows the ball to arrive and then clips it square on the leg-side
117ks slower ball around leg; Priyansh gently turns it to mid-wicket
138.3ks yorker outside off; dips right near the crease; Priyansh thinks he can get there on the full and attempts the drive; the ball sneaks under his bat
off-pace delivery; Priyansh pats it with soft hands to the off-side
four dots after the boundary. Another 141.4ks leg-lined delivery; giving Priyansh no room to work with; gently clipped to mid-wicket
slower and at the stumps; Prabhsimran nudges it into the vacant mid-wicket pocket

ball_event: out Caught by Deepak Chahar!! Ghazanfar strikes second ball. That's the wicket Mumbai Indians needed especially after that expensive first over. Flighted delivery
event_info: but the length is too full and that does not allow Priyansh to get under the whip; he tries his best to clear mid-wicket but hits it straight to Chahar; simple catch and Mumbai Indians have a wicket in the powerplay. Priyansh Arya c Deepak Chahar b AM Ghazanfar 15(9) [4s-2 6s-1]
93ks shortens the length and drifts it in; Connolly punches it to extra cover
flatter delivery around leg; Connolly tucks it straight to mid-wicket
sliding across the left-hander; Connolly pushes it off the outer half to cover
92ks fraction short and outside off; Connolly cracks it straight to Bosch at cover-point

dropped! That's a sitter and Bumrah does not hang on. But what is he doing at backward point? Slower short of length delivery; Prabhsimran tries his best to get on top of the bounce and cut; but does not keep it down and hits it to the left of Bumrah. He moves across and gets both hands but does not hang on
nicely done! Back of a length and angled across; Connolly uses that angle and steers it behind square; split short third and backward point perfectly
very full delivery; close to yorker length; Connolly digs it back to Hardik
thumped! Back of a length delivery; but there was width on offer; Connolly stays rooted to the crease and pumps it down the ground. Nice; clean strike and the ball sails over the rope
off-pace short of length delivery; Connolly drops his hands and lets it pass
134.9ks full toss on off; Connolly slices the drive behind square and deep backward point tidies up

poor delivery and Ghazanfar is made to pay. He strays down leg and the length too is on the shorter side; Connolly uses his wrists and whips it over long-leg. Right off the middle and the ball sails into the crowd..
flatter and drifting across; Connolly stays leg-side and knocks it to cover

ball_event: out Caught by Quinton de Kock!! Appeal for caught behind and Quinton too was confident
event_info: Mohit Krishnadas raises his finger. Connolly wasn't sure; has a chat with Prabhsimran and decides not to waste a review. Good call because UltraEdge showed a spike. Fired full and outside off; Connolly is drawn into the shot but is a tad late; gets a feather of an edge and de Kock does well behind the stumps. He was not keeping when the innings started; but has taken over from Rickelton. Cooper Connolly c Quinton de Kock b AM Ghazanfar 17(12) [4s-1 6s-2]

Iyer is up and running with a boundary. Ghazanfar missses his length - full pitcher outside off; Iyer does not need a second invitation; he leans into the drive and creams it through the packed off-side field
flatter delivery at the stumps; Iyer tucks it through mid-wicket and races back for the second
slower and drifting in; Iyer nurdles it backward of square and retains strike
137.7ks angling in from back of a length; Iyer turns it with soft hands towards mid-wicket

that's poor from Ghazanfar. Mumbai Indians can't afford such sloppy work. Back of a length and outside off; Iyer steers it to third man and it goes straight to Ghazanfar; he makes a mess and allows the ball to trickle into the rope
slower ball banged into the wicket; Iyer is half hearted into the pull and the top-edge balloons over the vacant short fine; just a single
139.1ks bouncer right at the batter; Prabhsimran ducks and allows it to sail over him
short of length delivery around off; Prabhsimran hops to get on top of the bounce and fends it with soft hands to backward point

shot! Width on offer and Prabhsimran cashes in. Back of a length outside off; he rides the bounce and punches it in front of square. There was a big gap between backward point and cover; and the ball raced away..
short and pounded into the wicket; Iyer ramps it fine and third man tidies up
on a length and outside off; Prabhsimran meets it on the up and drives it in front of square; easy couple
off-pace delivery outside off; Prabhsimran tries his best to reach it but can't; wide signalled
full pitcher around off; Prabhsimran drives it down to long-off
slower ball sprayed outside off; Iyer can't reach it and it is called a wide. Thakur wants it reviewed; but he was not serious
knuckle ball and not sure Iyer spotted it; he's early into the shot and has to check it; chips it towards mid-off and the ball lands safely. It was a chance if the fielder was inside the ring
117.3ks off-pace short of length delivery; Prabhsimran dabs it to the left of backward point
119.6ks slower ball outside off; Iyer goes hard on the cut and chops it back to Quinton de Kock
122.1ks slower ball bowled into the pitch; Prabhsimran plays a controlled pull in front of square and picks up a couple

Chahar continues to travel. They know he's the weak link in this attack and decide to take him on. Prabhsimran charges down the pitch; gives himself room and launches it over long-off. That ended up in the second tier of the Wankhede..
Chahar letting all the pressure go. Another freebie on the pads and Prabhsimran goes for finesse over power; he waits for the ball to arrive and glances it to fine leg
off-pace short of length delivery; Prabhsimran pulls to deep square leg
123.5ks slower ball banged in; Iyer was trying to ramp it over short third; the ball hits him on the glove and lobs up; lands in front of the incoming backward point fielder
back of a length delivery; Iyer flicks it to deep mid-wicket and retains strike
tossed up on off; Iyer gently turns it to the right of Ghazanfar
carrom ball which is full and outside off; Iyer drives to long-off
drifting into leg; Prabhsimran stays back and nurdles it round the corner - backward square leg
sliding in with the angle; Iyer clips it towards short fine and Bumrah dives to make the stop
leg-lined delivery; Prabhsimran flicks it to deep mid-wicket
low full toss around off; Iyer drives it straight back and the ball deflects off the stumps at the non-striker's end. There was a run-out check but Ghazanfar hadn't got a hand or finger to the ball
full toss outside off; Iyer slices the drive towards sweeper cover

hammered! Slower ball outside off; Prabhsimran saw the width and didn't hold back; drills the drive to the right of extra cover and gives Rutherford no chance. That was uppish but Rutherford was not catching that..

now just over short third. Prabhsimran is teasing the fielders but is getting away. Slower ball dug into the surface; Prabhsimran waits and slashes hard; gets more of a top-edge and has enough to clear the inner ring
full and angled into the pads; Prabhsimran flicks it to deep mid-wicket
leg-lined delivery; Iyer clips it between deep mid-wicket and deep square leg; easy couple
back of a length close to off; Iyer angles it with an open bat face to backward point

what was Ghazanfar doing? The ball goes right next to him and he does not move. Prabhsimran charges down and has to reach for the short of length delivery; toe-ends it to the left of short third and Ghazanfar did not spot it. He turns around and goes once the ball has gone past him. Suggests he didn't see it
sprays the slower ball outside off; Prabhsimran can't reach it and Quinton de Kock dives across to glove the ball

fifty for Prabhsimran. He's been consistent this season. He was dropped early in the innings and he's making Mumbai Indians pay. Every game he has gone past 35. Fraction short and he's quick to pick the length; swivels around and nails the pull to deep mid-wicket. He raises his bat and soaks in the applause of his team mates
fullish delivery; Prabhsimran drives it down the ground to long-off

Hardik is fuming. Iyer decides to have a piece of Thakur and sends him into the stands. The wide line does not work. Iyer was expecting that and shuffled across; stayed deep in the crease and launched it over deep extra cover. Punjab Kings are running away with the game..
full toss close to off; Iyer moves across and drives to long-on
not quite short and hence Prabhsimran mistimes the pull to deep mid-wicket
boundaries continue to flow.. Ghazanfar drifts it into the right-hander; Prabhsimran moves inside the line and sits down; laps it over short fine and collects the boundary
flighted outside off; Prabhsimran drives it down to long-off
that's poor from Ghazanfar. He goes round the wicket and sprays it down leg; Iyer could pick his spot and he does just that; helps it along backward of square and there's no point running for that
leg-lined delivery; Iyer tucks it to deep mid-wicket
low full toss outside off; Prabhsimran slices the drive to sweeper cover
drifting in; Iyer makes room and swings hard; drags it off the inner half to long-on

1 run; slower-ball off-cutter and short of length just outside off; Shreyas Iyer leans back and dabs to third man. And guess what - Jasprit Bumrah has overstepped. It will be a free-hit coming up for Prabhsimran Singh next
right up there; on off; Prabhsimran Singh clears his front leg and drives it hard and straight to extra-cover; the free-hit is a dot
and next ball is a a juicy low full-toss outside off; Prabhsimran Singh has it easy as he gets forward and drives it hard and wide of extra-cover; to his left; races away
another low full-toss; was the attempted yorker on off; Prabhsimran Singh gets forward and drives straight to the fielder at sweeper cover

what a shot that is. Look at the faces in the crowd. It's short outside off; Shreyas Iyer goes on the back foot and nails the pull over deep mid-wicket; went 10 rows back into the crowd. The faces in the crowd tell a story - they can't believe what is happening
slower-ball and short outside off; too high as Shreyas Iyer lets it go. Over the head

seam-up and full outside off; Shreyas Iyer gets forward to drive; the thick outside-edge could've been a catch to short third man but he is back and it's one even as Deepak Chahar slides to his left; he fumbles but it's just one
low full-toss on middle; Prabhsimran Singh gets forward and flicks to deep square-leg
it's a half-volley and Prabhsimran Singh is in the mood tonight. He gets forward and smashes it straight down the ground. Always four there
into the crowd. Full outside off; Prabhsimran Singh gets forward and lofts through the line; it flies all the way over long-off. Into the crowd. He just relied on timing
good short delivery. It's a wide. It's over the head of Prabhsimran Singh as he tries to pull; flies over the head and it's a wide
full on off; Prabhsimran Singh gets forward and drives it hard and straight to long-off
good yorker on off; Shreyas Iyer gets forward and digs it out; to the vacant cover-point area. Easy single
full on off; Prabhsimran Singh wants to drive through extra-cover; but the ball comes off a thick inside half and rolls to long-on
yorker length and just outside off; Shreyas Iyer digs it out to long-on
slower-ball and short of length outside off; Shreyas Iyer goes on the back foot and pulls it wide of deep square-leg; there was a fielder on the fence but it was well wide of the man
yorker length and sliding down leg; Shreyas Iyer flicks to deep backward square-leg; straight to the fielder
slower-ball and short outside off; Prabhsimran Singh goes on the back foot and opens the face of the bat to glide it; Chahar runs in from fine third man to catch but it falls short
low full-toss on middle; Shreyas Iyer is on fire. 50 for him. He gets forward and flicks wide of mid-wicket; runs away for four. Any erring in line and length; and it's four
short of length and nips back in to middle; Shreyas Iyer goes on the back foot and nudges it; on to the thigh pad as the thick inside-edge; rolls to the vacant forward short-leg area
slower-ball and short of length on the stumps; Prabhsimran Singh makes room to pull; misses. Bounces over the stumps. A rare dot

Naman Dhir the man at deep mid-wicket. He blames the lights. Sullen faces in the MI camp. They are shell-shocked. It's a catch and it's travelling at him with pace. He goes reverse-cup and the ball just about touches the fingertips and goes over. He was going back and should've caught that. Like the others; as the ball goes over; he has a look at the lights and gestures he didn't pick that
into the third-tier at deep mid-wicket. A half-volley on off; Shreyas Iyer with a clean clean loft. He gets forward and lofts; the ball sails over the fence. What a shot

ball_event: out Caught by Naman Dhir!! Now Dhir makes amends for that drop. He dives forward at deep mid-wicket and catches this. The round the wicket angle works for Shardul Thakur. It's a half-volley on the legs
event_info: Shreyas Iyer shuffles across and flicks; it's flat and going down; Dhir dives forward and takes it with two hands. He doesn't celebrate. Some joy for MI. Shreyas Iyer c Naman Dhir b Shardul Thakur 66(35) [4s-5 6s-4]
way too high this short delivery and Marcus Stoinis lets it go. Called a wide
low full-toss on middle; Marcus Stoinis gets forward to drive to long-on
low full-toss outside off; Prabhsimran Singh gets forward and drives it wide of long-off
full on middle; Stoinis gets forward and drills it hard and straight to long-on
way outside off; the slower-ball and Marcus Stoinis cuts and misses; called a wide
another slower-ball outside off; short of length and Marcus Stoinis goes on the back foot to cut; misses; this is well inside the tramline; so a dot
low full-toss outside off; Marcus Stoinis gets forward to drive and the thick inside-edge misses off-stump and goes well wide of Quinton de Kock to his left; runs away fine for four
way outside off; seam-up and past the tramline outside off; left alone and wide called

PBKS thump MI at the Wankhede. Shreyas and the PBKS fans are so happy. This is a a juicy low full-toss outside off; Marcus Stoinis gets forward and drives; more off the thick inside half; beats long-on to his right; needed a dive but the fielder didn't put that
